name: Tom Collins

spec.method_detail: Shake gin, lemon, and syrup with ice. Strain into ice-filled Collins glass. Top with club soda. Stir gently.

history: One of the great tall drinks. Jerry Thomas published a recipe in his 1876 bar guide. 'Tom' refers to Old Tom gin, a slightly sweeter style popular in the 19th century before London Dry became dominant. There was also a popular 1870s American prank — convincing someone that a man named 'Tom Collins' had been talking trash about them in another bar, sending them on a wild chase.
